text:
At first I thought this was the greatest razor ever. It provides a smooth shave, probably the best shave I've ever encountered. I have very sensitive skin, so I have to be selective. First impressions: It is a very sleek and cool looking razor. It is so fancy, that I am afraid of breaking it every time I touch it. The problem with this razor is not its performance, rather it is the maintenance. The lotion is expensive, and to make things worse, it is nearly impossible to find, unless you are willing to spend lots of money and buy it online. But, the razor works, so I stuck it out.

Next problem: Norelco suggests that you replace the cartridge for sanitary reasons each time you refill the lotion. OK, but when I ordered the lotion (which is supposed to come with the cartridge), THERE WAS NO CARTRIDGE! So, I decided to stick it out anyway. Each time I replaced the lotion, I would just remove the cartridge and clean it myself. That seemed fine, until this morning when I accidentally broke a piece of this fragile cartridge. Now, I can't get the lotion to come out, so the razor is ineffective. I tried to buy a new lotion with cartridge at Target, and the lotion came without the cartridge, so I have to return it.

I know Phillips-Norelco has excellent customer service, so I called to see if they could help me. It turns out that the lotion does not always come with the cartridge (although it is advertised that way and I was told that it came with the cartridge by a customer service rep before I bought the razor). So, it's kind of like a little guessing game. Where can I find the lotion and cartridge together? Fun! I asked if they could just send me some cartridges, no lotion, just cartridges and I'd be happy. But they could not do that, so I am going to return this razor and get my money back.

I still have a previous Norelco shaver (dry) and I will try that again, although it is not as good. I think in the end I may have to resort back to the painful manual razor shave. I have been a Norelco customer for many years and up to this point I was always very pleased, this is disappointing.

To summarize: This is an excellent razor, but it is not worth the money or the hassle you have to go through to make it work effectively!

I welcome any shaving advice to help my sensitive skin!

text: As a woman in her 30s, I get the occasional chin hair (or 5) so I bought these wax strips to help. The strips are very large so I just cut a small piece to use. text:
As a long time Sonicare user, I've stuck to the brand due to the good experiences I've had with them. Any time I've had to go back to a regular tooth brush or a disposable electric toothbrush, it just wasn't the same. So, it was time go to back to a sonic toothbrush, so decided to give the Panasonic a try. I'm convinced that this is a worthy competitor in the sonic toothbrush market.

The first thing you notice after brushing your teeth is the fresh from the dentist office feeling. Aided with flossing, it works really well on plaque and also makes your teeth quite bright and sparkling.

The head is quite comfortable and seems durable. As of this review, I've not seen options for replacing the heads.

The only thing I would give a mild negative mark to would be the timer. It doesn't shut off but gives you reminders like every 30 seconds for like the first two minutes, which may indicate which side to brush for 30 seconds. After two minutes, it seems to do it every 2 minutes. Depending on your preferences, the 30 seconds per side work well.

I can highly recommend this toothbrush against other comparable brands. Quite surprised...

text: I have split opinions on this flat iron. If you are ironing dry hair and like a wider plate (this one is 1.25”), it does a nice job straightening. However, as far as working on wet hair, it is terrible. Both my college-aged daughter and I both tried it on wet hair and both of us came to the same conclusion. While the iron did dry my wet hair, it also caused it to be frizzy, for lack of a better word. After using it, my hair was not smooth and shiny like it is after I iron my already blown dry hair. Instead my hair looked “damaged” and rough around the edges. I have naturally wavy hair that is thick. Perhaps this would work better on someone with thinner, straighter hair. I use a flat iron after every time I blow dry my hair and thought this would be a good shortcut. Nope. I’m sticking with my regular process of blowing dry first and ironing afterward. I will likely keep this iron as my back-up since it does a perfectly fine job once my hair is already dry.

text:
I was looking for a mascara that would give my lashes more volume and appear thicker. I happily don't need them to be any longer, but I'll take a bit of length if I can get thicker, fuller looking lashes as well. Well, not so much. The product glommed onto several lashes at a time and glued them together with a frightening stiffness that made me feel like I was constantly surprised. I felt like I had my eyes wide open all the time. The little clumps of lashes were no thicker than my naked lashes, so that didn't work for me. They did get a bit longer so that they brushed against my glasses all the time. I hate when that happens.

I was able to wear it overnight without messing up the pillow case and it did not leave me looking like a raccoon in the morning. When it came to to wash my face, though, the warm water made the mascara come off in "tubes" that stretched and clung to my cheeks. It was icky.

I was not pleased with the results of this mascara. I don't want to use it anymore. If you want longer eyelashes and a product that will withstand overnight use, then you might consider this mascara. If you don't mind peeling off stretchy black tubes of mascara when you wash your face, then you might consider this mascara. If you want thicker, fuller looking lashes, this product will not be your best choice.

text:
This is a truly versatile product. It can be used in cooking or for external uses (as an astringent, vitamin C enriched application). I thought the value would be for adding an organic orange flavor aspect to cooking/baking. However, I was enlightened by the significant other that it would make for an excellent face mask/scrub. Knowing that i'd eventually lose the argument, I simply divided the package into half, so we both could utilize this powder.
I do like that they included a bag clip, to help keep the foil pack airtight and watertight. This facilitates the longevity and safety of the powder. Still, I put my share into a sealable glass jar (dark colored to minimize sunlight exposure).
I sprinkled some of this powder into green tea, and it 'bloomed' into a tasty, tangy orange delight. it's a pleasant way to get a bit more Vitamin C, as well as any other flavonoids associated with orange peels.
It is true you can zest a fresh orange, or dry orange peels (my mother used to dry tangerine peels, to use in cooking asian cuisine, a tradition my sister carries on). However, having this ground orange peel ready to go is very convenient. And it's USDA Organic.
I will give some of this powder to my sister, perhaps hinting she could cook something and give me some. haha.
Besides being a time saver/convenient, the quantity is more than ample when you consider the amount of dried orange rind that is necessary to deliver quantity in powdered form.
I feel this orange peel powder merits a solid 4.5 star rating, with a very high level recommendation.

text: I enjoyed this product so much I decided to buy another tube because my current one is running low. After suffering from the occasional underarm irritation from using regular deodorants and antiperspirants over the years I decided to try something new, and this product caught my eye. I did some research on it and knew about the possible skin irritation that might occur when I began using it, but luckily for me I never got any skin irritation from this product. I know people might think Primal Pit Paste is expensive for a deodorant, but I think the price is on par with traditional deodorants. At $10.95 I am still using the original tube I purchased 5 months ago and I have no doubt that it will make it to 6 months, now compare that to the price of regular deodorant/antiperspirants that cost $5 to $6 a stick and only last maybe 3 months. The scent of Royal and Rogue is great, and long lasting. That is another reason why I prefer this to regular deodorants. After a while with traditional deodorants after you sweat the mixture of the sweat and the fragrance of the deodorant becomes quite unpleasant. With Primal Pit Paste, since it neutralizes the bacteria that makes the sweat stink, you don't get that foul odor when the fragrance and sweat mix.

text:
There have been a lot of complaints about the vacuum option of this trimmer. Let's be realistic....its not a Hoover, it's a trimmer. It catches MOST of the hairs, which is a lot better than NONE of the hairs. I've used it a few times so far and haven't had the problem others complained about where the setting is changed mid-trim. Just hold the thing below the dial, and not right on the dial. It gave a nice even trim all around. I've had it for a couple of months, used it 3-4 times, and still haven't had to recharge it.

Suggested improvement - A stand would have been great so I could put it in my medicine cabinet and not have it roll around my counter.

Tips for using - I found you're better off with dry hair. When the hair's wet, it's harder for the vacuum to pull it in. I also found that the vacuum works much better on longer hair then on shorter hair. Maybe the shorter hairs are too light and get blown around? Last but not least, watch out for the settings. The dial goes 1-9 so I started with 4, which was way too short. I could have grown that beard from scratch in a couple of weeks. 5 also proved a little short for me. text:
My daughter and I discovered facial sheet masks over the summer and have become fans of them. They are easy and fun to use. This box of Anti-Aging sheet masks comes with 5 masks. Each mask is individually wrapped. When you open up a mask and remove it from the packing, you see that there are 2 layers. One is the sheet mask which is thin and papery like. The other is a plastic type material. The two layers are together, so I put both of them on my face. The other masks that I have used didn't have this plastic layer. I couldn't get it to lay flat on my skin very well, so I peeled the plastic layer off leaving just the paper one. This was what I was more familiar with. That layer was easy to mold to my face. The mask was very cold which felt refreshing. It's very wet - not to the point of dripping off my face, but enough that you want to spread it around more on the mask as it is on your face. It says to leave it on for 15-20 minutes. I opted for the lesser time because my coffee was ready & I didn't want to wait any longer for it! Then you are supposed to rub any left over "goo" (for a lack of a better word) into you skin. I accidently got a little into my eye, but it didn't burn or sting at all, which surprised me. I have very sensitive eyes and was expecting to feel some pain. I regularly use anti-aging products, plus I have only used one of these sheet masks so far, so I don't notice any difference to my skin, but I would definitely add this to my beauty routine.

*I purchased these masks at a reduced price for the purpose of trying out & reviewing. My thoughts & opinions are my own.

text: My husband and I are so in love with his Wild Willie’s Beard Elixir, so we were incredibly excited for his Wild Willie’s Beard Wash and Beard Rinse. text:
Dec 2019: (1st review, see follow up at the bottom)
I read most of the negative reviews to somewhat prepare on the expected... I've had the item for 5 months now. I like the upper storage area a lot. It holds all my polishes and brushes. The adapter seems to be holding strong.

The light is very strong, be cautious By wearing anti UV ray gloves and/or sunblock. The color of hands (and I’m taking about my skin not nail color) is much darker because of how strong this light is! If I leave my hand under the light longer than 15-20 seconds I can feel the heat. So I cure the gel in small pauses. Also, the 30 sec and 90 sec button timer sucks. I use my phone to time it.

Overall, the machine gets the job done.
**********************************************************

Jan 2021 Edit:
I have two years with this item. The wire continues to work well and the lighting system is as strong as day one- I’ve not changed any bulbs. I still haven’t gotten the hang out of the timer but the light is so darn strong the it cures the gel within a few seconds. Be cautious ladies, it does burn/tan the hands.

I recently purchased the apres system and this item is super helpful because the bottom section (where the hand sits) can be removed. I place the light over the edge of the desk and voila! It begins curing the gel from a distance.

text:
I had very high hopes for this cleanser. I used this cleanser in combination with the Antioxidant Toner, which is also manufactured by Isabel Fay. I have used other Isabel Fay products in the past that I have thought very highly of, so I was quite disappointed that I didn't love this product.

First, the positives: this cleanser smells amazing. I love the light rose-petal smell. The cleanser feels a little thin when it comes out of the pump, and I felt like I needed to use a little more than I would with other brands in order to get a good lather. It removed my makeup and did not leave my skin feeling dry. I followed the cleanser with the Antioxidant Toner.

Now, for the negatives: these products have caused my acne-prone skin to have the worst breakout I have had in a very long time. I went from mostly clear skin to 10+ blemishes, most of which are cystic in nature. I changed nothing else about my skincare routine, so I can only conclude that the Isabel Fay products do not agree with my skin.

I believe that the ingredients are high-quality, but perhaps this product would be best suited for people with normal-dry skin rather than my acne-prone skin type. I cannot recommend this to people with acne-prone skin. I am currently in damage-control mode, trying to get my skin to return to calm and treat the breakouts. I noticed that this cleanser contains many high-quality oils; this should have been a red flag to me, since I know that most oils will cause precisely the type of results I experienced with my skin. A change in labeling to reflect the recommended skin types for this product could possibly prevent other people from experiencing the same unhappy results that I did.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest review. All opinions expressed are my own.

text:
Let’s see. I was looking for a nice, inexpensive mint green pallet. It’s hard to find a genuine mint green. No one had left a review of this, so I decided to take a chance. First of all, the shipping took forever. I ordered this April 25, and I didn’t receive it until May 22, almost a month later. I’ve received things faster ordering them from China! Then, once I got it, I tried it out (see pics). The colors are nice, but not extremely impressive. The matte colors are very powdery and get all over your face. The shimmers are nice. I usually dust my eyes with powder, after I apply eyeshadow, to set it, and then apply more shadow, if I need it. I lightly dusted loose powder in my eyes, after application, and I dust very gently. Most of the shadow came off. So I reapplied. The color was nice, but the matte shades faded a lot, after two hours. The shimmer stayed, but the pigment wasn’t as strong, after a few hours.

So do yourself a favor. Buy a mint green palette from a store where you can get it faster, and where the color payoff is better.

text:
My 2nd Sonicare toothbrush died, so I picked up this Oral-B. For what I paid, I guess I can't complain.

There are things I prefer about my Sonicare, like the fact that it had a charging indicator, it was much quieter, and seemed more powerful.

For what I paid for this Oral-B, I guess I can't complain. It does the job, although it is much louder than my Sonicare. I have noticed that the toothbrush seems to "bog down" if you use anything but light pressure. I mean it get's a bit bogged down when it's rubbing up against my cheek when brushing my back molars. There is no charging indicator on the unit, so there's no way of knowing whether it is charging or not. I will say, however, I do prefer the brush head size/shape in comparison to Sonicare's shape/size. I was even using the compact brush head's with my Sonicare, but the Oral-B's are just more comfortable and do a better job reaching my back molars. While I wouldn't buy this model again, I will most likely step up to a higher priced model to see if the noise gets any quieter and the motor stronger. All in all, it was worth it to find out I prefer the brush head shape/size over Sonicare's. Also, the price of the replacement brushes are much more reasonable. Granted you change these heads ever 3 mths v. 6 mths with the Sonicare, it's still a significant savings.

text:
This cream was just ok for me. I bought this after my most recent nighttime moisturizer went out (Cetaphil) because I'm trying to shift my skin care regimen to more natural ingredients now that I'm pregnant, but I don't think it was any more moisturizing than anything else I've used in the past, and it definitely didn't seem to make my acne-prone skin any better. The scent was fine for me. (I was still using my Cerave with SPF for my daytime cream because I didn't want to throw it away.)

I ended up switching my moisturizers just three days ago to Bee Naturals Best Oil Free Moisturizer for both my day (with sunblock - Blue Lizard - Australian Sunscreen Face Daily Moisturizer Fragrance Free 30 SPF) and night time moisturizer (by itself), and this has worked out ten times better for me. Instead of waking up each morning discovering new cystic acne bumps and a scattering of blackheads all over my cheeks, or using 2-6 oil blotting sheets during the day, my face has felt clean yet moisturized, and I think my acne-prone skin is finally getting better!

In summary, I think AquaTica Organic Botanical Night Cream is fine and can be good for a lot of people, but it wasn't the game changer for me -- I have eczema, I'm pregnant, I have acne-prone skin already that's gotten worse since pregnant, and my face is generally oily. I don't think it made things worse, it moisturized, the scent didn't bother me, and it did make me feel better that I was using something natural, but it didn't make my skin noticeably better, especially because I still broke out.

The ones that are in English, Solanum Melongena which is an eggplant extract, EDTA which is a chelating agent and not good for you at all, Camellia Sinensis Leaf which is a skin conditioner and perfume and CI 77492 which is a synthetic cosmetic colorant. There is a -20 which typically is a PEG which Polyethylene Glycol and can cause health issues if used in excess.

The application of the product itself is a neat concept and makes applying a mask much easier. There are unnecessary perfumes and colors in this product along with EDTA and possibly PEG which I stay away from.

Learn your skin and what it can handle also what you are looking for in a skin. This is not a natural product by any stretch of the imagination.

text:
I own this hair brush and one made buy a different brand. I got to say this one is not as good. The Hair Tamer brush does not have as many bristles as the other one I own. The bristles it does have are also sharp and scratch the scalp. My other brush does not do that. So did the Hair Tamer brush do a good job? Yes, but who wants your scalp scratched? I would not be surprised if it ends up kicking up dandruff since it is scrapping the skin.

I did test this brush out on some of my synthetic hair extensions. If you own hair pieces with synthetic hair you know they are a nightmare to untangle. I found that since this had fewer bristles than my other brush it made brushing out my extensions much easier. So this will probably be my main brush for hair extensions.

This is not a brush I can recommend since it hurts to use it. I don’t like the idea of having a brush sitting around that I only can use on extensions and not on my head. If the manufacture smooths the tips then this would be a five star brush.

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

Pros:

-Ergonomically designed handle
-Works wonderfully on synthetic hair extension
-Light weight
-Fits in the hand well
-Did a great job of detangling

Cons:

-Sharp bristles
-Item description says that it is great for children. I think you will have a very unhappy child if you try to use a sharp bristle brush on them.
-Will probably kick up dandruff since it scratches the scalp

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

I received this item for free for my honest and unbiased review of this hair brush. I never review a product that I have not tried and tested out myself. I rely on product reviews as much as you do when I make my buying decisions, and I hope you found this review helpful.

text:
This lipstick in Retro Bettie is a MUST HAVE for my beauty regimen as a jazz singer. I have to put my face on about an hour before I get to a gig, which is an hour before it starts, and it lasts 3 hours: so I need a lipstick that can last a minimum of 5 hours. THIS IS THAT LIPSTICK!

It goes on smooth, and the tip is like a typical lip gloss applicator. But it is not forgiving (it will temporarily stain your regular skin if you're not careful!), it is forever. Once you put it on just hang out with your mouth open for 30 seconds for it to seal in, and then you're good to go, with drinks and everything, for at least my usual 5-hour stint.

At the end of the day, this lipstick does smudge off, or even wash off: it flakes off. Which is kind of amazing since I don't use makeup remover: I just rub my lips against each other, or rub them with a paper towel or washclothes and the whole thing comes off easily.

NOTE: Your lips will feel dry by the end of the night because they will be, but you CANNOT add a lip gloss or chapstick atop this lipstick or it will completely lose its efficacy as a stay-put product: it will move around and stain everything—I learned this the hard way.

Almost every pic on my Instagram (@ojanmarie) has me wearing this excellent shade, which is a true red, on the slightly blue-red side, but not bright like a tomato, much deeper and more saturated than that. It will go on your lips a lighter shade and will dry darker, just FYI.

Good luck using my fav product!!!

text:
I had high hopes for this little baby, but after using for a few days, I must say it's just "okay". Perhaps it may be because when I bought it I had thick (or used to before my brush starting feasting on it) hair down to my butt and was tired of finding enough to knit my cat a sweater in my hair brush every morning. So I wanted something that was more user friendly. But then on an impulse I cut my naturally curly hair very short.

The Pros: It seems sturdy and well-made with a nice grain. It's smooth (the teeth on the plastic picks tend to splinter and peel with time). It feels comfortable to use. It probably has good stay power.

The Cons: By far the biggest for me is that the teeth are spaced much more widely apart and are much thicker than I expected from the photo. This means you can't really get the curl definition that the picks with a more narrow spacing will give you. I also wish the handle were a bit longer.

text: I really like this roller... it is so convenient and easy to apply (and also feels good doing it). It does provide some moisturization, without leaving a sheen, but doesn’t work as well as another serum that I have used (which also has a very similar roller type application). text:
I have many Majestic Pure products (from skin care products, to hair care, to essential oils), and have always been very satisfied. I am also pleased with the retinol cream. Having very sensitive skin, I tend to use products containing retinol quite sparingly (aka, every few days, vs. daily) as they tend to cause my skin to become quite irritated or scaly after a few days of daily use. I’ve been using this around just my eyes every evening (about 1 hour after applying a hyaluronic acid serum), as my skin is so sensitive that using anything with retinol on my entire face seems to really cause a lot of scaliness. And, this is where the fine lines are really beginning to show.

The results – after using a cream with retinol in combination with hyaluronic acid serum for 3 weeks, I’ve been noticing a definite reduction in the fine lines. They are still there but they were becoming really noticeable.

The cream has ingredients that are highly moisturizing (again, which I like to follow up on a serum with). The consistency is pretty thin (not thick and creamy like a lotion), and there is no noticeable fragrance. It seems to absorb quickly and is not oily.

Some note on the ingredients:

Retinol 2.5% - Retinol is another name for Vitamin A, which is an antioxidant (helps reverse the damage caused by free radicals, apparent in wrinkles and aging), encourages the production of collagen, and elastin.
Jojoba Oil - closely resembles sebum (a waxy substance produced by our skin glands), packed with vitamins and minerals - Vitamin E, B, copper, zinc, selenium, iodine and chromium.
Green Tea - Tea is high in anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, whether ingested or applied topically. Green tea contains tannins which can reduce puffiness and inflammation of the skin. It also contains polyphenols, which can combat free radicals (which contribute to aging). The catechins in tea can combat the bacteria that contribute to acne.
Propolis - Propolis, “bee glue or bee penicillin” is the resinous material gathered by honeybees from flowers and leaf buds of various plants. It contains dozens of anti-oxidants,
Shea Butter - The moisturizers in shea butter are similar to those produced by the skin’s sebaceous glands, making it one of the best choices for super-dry or cracked skin. Shea butter also contains anti-inflammatory compounds – great for applying to inflamed or irritated skin (think eczema, chapped skin, bug bites)
Vitamin B5 - A humectant (a water-binding substance that attracts and retains water). Particularly good for the skin as it stabilizes the skin barrier, increasing hydration and improving softness and elasticity.
Vitamin E - A powerful antioxidant, that neutralizes the oxidant effect of free radicals, which are molecules that damage collagen and cause skin dryness, fine lines and wrinkles

*I received the retinol cream for evaluation and review

text:
Updated review:
After a month of having these extensions I unfortunately need to change my review. These extensions came in earlier than planned, which was great. Packaging was okay, but the extensions were folded in half which isn’t anything a straightener can’t fix. I saw many other reviews saying the hair singes. I only used a straightener on them the first couple of weeks and it worked great and the extensions looked really good. Then I decided to curl them for a night out. After wearing them curled I brushed them out and attempted to straighten them again a few days later. Completely singed at the bottom. 4 inches worth of tangly, horrible ends. I trimmed them, and they look a little better but still singed and noticeably tangled. Maybe I’m missing something and it’s user error and I should’ve washed them before trying to re straighten them, but I wasn’t aware of it or even sure how accurate that is. I’ve worn extensions for several years now and this is the only pair I’ve had issues with. These extensions are worth the price. If you’re wanting extensions that can handle any type of heat you’ll have to spend a little more. These lasted a month, but I expected that.

text:
I had originally been purchasing Tom Ford Oud Wood cologne at a retail outlet and found that while expensive it is a great-smelling cologne and a cologne that lasts a long time on my skin and clothing. But I decided to try "Perfume Oils" instead of the actual cologne because the "Perfume Oils" are about 10 to 20 times cheaper than the actual cologne. So I ordered 8 oz, Brown - Bargz Perfume - Oud Wood Body oil. The oil arrived as Amazon said it would ... on time .. in perfect condition ... so I tried some of the product.

The oil smelled really wonderful in the container and it smelled really wonderful and authentically "Oud Wood" on my skin but it lasted on me for only about an hour and then the odor faded away to almost none at all. I do not blame the oil for this because all fragrances have their[[ASIN:B00XYM54TI 8 oz, Brown - Bargz Perfume - Oud Wood Body Oil For Men by Tom Ford Scented Fragrance]] own characteristics and all fragrances react with individual body chemistry in a different manner. Some fragrances last on individual users longer than others because of the effects of bodily chemistry. So if anything I suspect that my experience with the product is strictly based on my personal body chemistry and so my disappointment has nothing to do with the product. I will not blame the product. But I sense that I will have to return to using actual colognes instead of body oil formulations.

text: I bought two of these (blue and black) and returned both. I buy a luminous watch because there are times (like driving at night) when I need to see what time it is in the dark. I charged the lume of these watches on the window sill in direct sunlight for hours. Tested in a totally dark room, the luminescence was minimal to start, and I watched with disappointment as the lume quickly faded entirely away within a very few minutes. Otherwise, this watch is very nice and of high value. It should be noted that I am not one of those nitpickers that unreasonably expect a watch at this price point to have a minute hand that precisely hits every marker on the dial and all the other finest hallmarks of absolute perfection that one might expect of a jewelry timepiece costing ten or twenty times this price. My requirements for a watch are accurate time, durability and good lume. I cannot settle for two out of three.  So I gave it three stars out of five. I am surprised the poor lume was not mentioned more in other reviews. Perhaps I had two flukes, or lume is not important to others.

text:
My hematite magnetic bracelet arrived today, one day sooner than expected which is a good thing. The early arrival is the only reason this product got an extra star, I would have only given it 2 stars, not 3, but the early arrival helped saved its rating. I know the old adage "you get what you pay for" and I guess I will have to learn to deal with that in regards to this bracelet. Don't get me wrong, it's attractive, very masculine and has an awesome color. But the quality of it all, it would have been nice for them to put a couple more hematite pieces onto the bracelet. I'm not a very big guy, my family jokes about my girl looking wrists all the time. But even with my petite wrists the black stretchy bands that make up the bracelet are showing. I have like a half inch gap of missing material, meaning a half an inch of my skin and those two black cords are showing! It would have been great for them to put at least two more of those block shaped pieces and maybe 4 more pearls into the bracelet, then I wouldn't have this embarrassing gap on the back side of the bracelet. Oh well, I will wear it from time to time, but not as many times as I was was planning to before making this purchase.

(If you got big wrists, this bracelet may not be for you, choose another one from Amazon.com, maybe one this is bigger!)

text:
This is a beginner set, but it's not looking to help you-- No instructions.

Lucky for me--This isn't my first gel kit.

At first when I opened the box I liked how much they had to offer creatively. Some kits try to force a look, but this one was open to play with. Maybe not the best idea for a beginner kit- but I enjoyed that.

First the good:
I like this type of lamp when I'm doing gel- it's easy together both my hands in to hold the nail down when I first start, then I usually pick up the light and rotate it to get a full cure. Yes I have to restart the lamp a few times, but this isn't an expensive lamp so I don't mind.

I liked the little box they came in- sure it's cheep, but it was a nice tough to have (almost) everything in once place.

Then the annoying:

There are a lot of odd little problems, like the Gel itself, two are jelly clear and one is opaque. Opaque gel takes a little longer to cure because the light works a bit harder to travel though the nail. It would have been helpful if they mentioned that, OR only gave you one type of gel-so it doesn't confuse you-- but they didn't.

The nail forms were a bad fir for my hand. Thats just an unlucky break- all hands are diffrent- so im not including that in my rating, just something to mention in case they don't work for you too.

The colors were a little annoying to use, no color indicator on the bottle. this is common with cheeper sets, but I thought I'd mention it anyway- It can make doing your nails just a little bothersome.

The ugly-
they don't include slip solution-I ended up using my own from my nail kit, so I didn't have any problems, but that's a huge mistake on their part-- if you're just starting out you'll think gel nails are a sticky mess. Slip solution makes them manageable.

Overall- I feel like there are better kits that will give you a cleaner start over this one--

I hope this review was helpful!

text:
T970 is miles away from its predecessor(s) (I also own a T7500 Deluxe) as far as the feature set goes. It boasts a charge level indicator, incorporated vacuum, and fast 15-minute charging time. However, I don't know what was in the mind of the industrial designers who came up with this latest installment from Norelco/Philips other than capitalize on the truck craze that's taken such a powerful hold on us. Indeed, I wish all those miles were for the right reason. Let me explain:

I view the newly added vacuum function adding only marginal benefit to this unit, at the expense of draining battery power fast, noise making, and increased diameter size. With T7500, all one had to do was to charge the trimmer every other 15th use or so, and undo a couple of things to clean it after each use. Also, T7500 was smaller, it fit well in your hand and/or travel pouch, and could be charged at 110V-240V. Now, T970 requires a recharge every 3rd use or so, and it's so much harder to fit in you hand and/or in your travel bag since it has a larger diameter and comes with a power adaptor that works only at 110V. Cleaning T970 is akin to undoing a Matrioshka, the Russian nesting doll.

Thinking more about it, I came to the conclusion that T970 wasn't put together by industrial designers but some marketing types... For these guys I would venture to suggest that they should keep T7500 on the market; it would serve very well the traveller types, whereas its successor would be for stay-at-home folks.

Considering it sells at a discount and it comes with a 60-day return period, I am able to rate it cautiously at 3 stars.

text:
I'm a bit of a shaving geek. I don't have a huge collection, but I do have "a collection". Straight razors, single edge, and even a couple double edge razors.

So my wife picked this up for me last Christmas.

It's not too shabby. Razor has a good feel in the hand, though I wish the handle was a bit shorter. The head is more aggressive than say, a Gillette Tech. Different feel and balance than a Super Speed.

A Feather or Polsilver Super Iridium makes for a decent shave.

The brush is ok. A bit stiff for "badger", but not as stiff as a Boar Brush. Looks good in the set at least, but I'm still getting loose hairs out of it.

The stand: The stand is a bit lacking. A heavier base would have helped, and none of my other brushes fit "quite right" in the stand. It was certainly designed for the included hardware.

text:
The scent is delightful, like laundry detergent. I also like that it's easy to wash off and doesn't contain aluminum, but the positives end there. After applying, it takes about 5-10 minutes to dry sufficiently before putting on clothes, and even then it doesn't fully dry until hours later. It slightly decreases the amount of odor that's released compared to no deodorant, but really not by much; I'd say for me it decreases odor by about 30%. (And I'm not sure it's not just a masking effect at that.) Within an hour of putting it on, I can smell odor, just sitting at my desk in an air-conditioned office. It's better than nothing, but I've definitely used better aluminum-free deodorants. (Secret makes the best one I've used by far, and Jason makes a decent tea tree one. Native works really well but feels really thick and stains clothes, so I don't use it.)

I'll only be using it to chill at home when I'm alone; I won't be wearing it if I'm going to be around others.

text:
So far, I have no real complaints about this NIRA Skincare Laser & Hyaluronic Acid Serum Bundle. I’m still at the point where I’m not sure if I can say it truly works because I need to use it for a longer period of time, but I’ll discuss various aspects about the product that will hopefully help with your decision-making.

-Came well-packaged in a small, but sturdy cardboard box. Everything arrived intact. I have continued to use this box for storage of the product.
-Comes with the handheld laser device, charger with base to plug into an outlet, and manual
-No complaints about the battery life so far
-Simple to use: there are two buttons: The power button that turns the device on. Then you can press it to cycle through the various levels of intensity 1-5 which are clearly shown using green light bars. You can then hold the power button down to turn the device off again. The top button can be pressed to activate the laser after you have chosen the level of intensity you want.
-The laser only works if positioned correctly over an area of skin. It will give a single negative sounding beep if not positioned correctly, and a positive sounding double beep if it is positioned correctly as the laser works. You wait to move the device to the next area after you hear that second beep indicating that it is finished with that laser sequence
-The device has a white, plastic outer shell. It is lightweight while still feeling sturdy and durable. I would still be very careful not to drop this as I’m not sure how it could handle a fall
-I would recommend experimenting on the lower intensity settings. The undereye area is pretty sensitive. I feel I have a pretty high pain tolerance, but my eyes often still tear up after using per instructions on the highest settings of 4 and 5. For whatever reason, my left eye is much more sensitive to this.
-I don’t want to say it is truly painful, but I suppose the best way to describe the sensation is almost similar to if you were being stung by a rubber band that you pulled back and let hit your skin if that makes sense. It isn’t comfortable, but you do get more used to this and can work your way up to a higher level of intensity. You will probably also feel some warmth on the skin after using.
-I know this is marketed for under the eyes, but I have also been using on my forehead and by my smile lines.

***The instruction manual is pretty clear and straightforward. You definitely need to read this before getting started. Here is some information that you can find within the manual:

-They say all skin tones and anyone over the age of 12 can use NIRA to reduce the appearance of wrinkles around the eyes
-You need to clean and dry your face just prior to using NIRA
-It can be using morning or night and up to twice daily. They recommend waiting 1 minute before retreating each area and not to treat more than twice within a 1-hour period.
-Do not treat the same spot twice in a row, even if the pulse is rejected and you get the low pitched, negative sounding beep
-The instructions clearly show you the recommended way to treat the undereye area and the treatment pattern to use
-They recommend using the highest power level where you feel warmth without pain
-You should use a facial moisturizer after each treatment and a daily sunscreen with at least SPF 30 is recommended. This bundle includes the hyaluronic acid serum which can also be used.
-Device should be kept clean and free of dust, especially the glass lens which can be cleaned with a cotton swab. Surfaces can be cleaned with 70% alcohol solution between each use.
-They say some potential side effects include: skin warmth, redness, and stinging. Less frequent side effects include dryness, roughness, tanned appearance to skin, swelling, flaking, itching, crusting, bruising, pimple-like rash, irritated skin, blistering, scarring, infection, and skin color changes where your skin may become darker or lighter. I have only experienced the temporary warmth and stinging during use- no redness or anything else.
-The company also cautions those who have skin conditions or are undergoing professional cosmetic treatments such as injectable toxins like Botox or fillers- saying to check with your doctor or clinician prior to using NIRA.
-Only use on intact skin
-Don’t use if pregnant or nursing or undergoing fertility treatments
-Do not use if you have a history of skin cancer, any other cancer, or pre-malignant moles
-Do not treat if you have tanned skin which is apparently at increased risk of adverse effects with light-based devices

Overall, I’m happy with how the product functions and the ease of use. I will continue using and will update if I have any further comments or concerns. I haven’t noticed any real results yet, but I also haven’t been using it that long. One question that comes to mind is this: What is the lifespan of this device? Months? Years? Or does it give out after firing the laser a certain number of times? I could have missed it in the manual, but that is something I’m still not sure of. For the current price of $329, that is something I think people may want to know. I haven’t had any issues with the serum that comes in this package, but it seems pretty simple and basic.

text:
I had never seen a clip like this before so thought I'd try it out. Look closely at the product pictures; this involves clipping some hair in the bottom, larger clip while leaving loose hair in front, and then dividing the front portion of hair into three parts and clipping into the three smaller, top clips. Very interesting!
It actually looks cuter than I expected, but:
-the bottom clip is fairly large and definitely for thicker hair; even the top clips are large-ish considering they are meant to hold just a smaller, 1/3 size section of hair. So not really made for those with fine/thin hair, including small children with wispy hair.
-the quality of these is not awesome. They are very much junky black plastic with glued-on rhinestones. There are strings of dried glue on mine--easily pulled off, but still. Best for maybe a costume party or a child or someone with very dark hair that will camouflage the black plastic so that only the flower clips are noticeable.

text:
Like the headlines says, it still has a smell like old lady lotion (reminds me of old school oil of olay my grandmother loved), but more of a lavender scent when hair dries. While not too strong compared to others out there, it’s still disappointing when you wanted something with no smell. The majority of the smell came from the shampoo and didn’t notice as much of a smell coming from the conditioner so the conditioner might be usable.

As others complained, the cap arrived loose so the shampoo did leak. Fortunately it was wrapped fairly well (bottles individually wrapped and then the 2 bottles shrink wrapped together) so damage to the other items in the shipment was minimal. I would recommend ordering it separately.

text:
This lipstick set comes in 2 sets of 5, Set A and Set B. I wound up only opening the "A" set, as after trying these 5 and finding only 2 to work for me, I decided to give the "B" set away. From the photos, I thought that the colors would be nice and muted, but that's not really the case. The color I am wearing in the photo is "Almost Friday," which is a pretty neutral, nude-ish pink. I also kept "Blush for Me," a light, brownish peach. The red shade, "Red for Me," was very bright and didn't seem to belong to the rest of this collection. But the other shades seemed MUCH less intense than in the product photos. Rather than buying a set of 10 like this, I think most people would be better off choosing individual shades that work best for them.

Two other things to note. When I opened one of the lipsticks, the color cylinder popped out and onto my floor, so I wonder about quality. text:
I was so happy when these arrived. The stickers looks absolutely beautiful, a first glance I fell in love. The designs and the colors were great; the colors have an iridescent quality in addition to the wide range of colors, it really adds another layer.'

Now, what disappointed me. These don't stick, like at all. There is a small amount of stickiness that lasts for mere seconds before these things fall off their surface (I've tried plastic, acrylic, skin, hardwood, and even carpeting to see). Honestly I was pretty surprised that these didn't stick to my carpet fibers. You can see in my photo that I used these for nail art, and the lifting is visible. I held the stickers down when I noticed them lifting from the nail, but after a 30 seconds of holding the sticker down (onto a top coat, too) the gorgeous little butterflies lifted anyway.

I want to give this product more stars so badly. The three stars I have given are for the aesthetics alone, while the other two stars are missing because the product does not really work the way it is intended to. I would recommend buying these as long as you are aware that you're going to need another product to make these stick to whatever surface you are using, and that lifting of the sticker away from the surface can happen.

text:
These beads aren't really "beads" as I think of them, then are thin strip of metal bent into a circular shape. They are quite fragile, and will split in half if opened too far. These were suggested to me by a friend who has her dreads decked out in jewelry and decorations, so I figured she knew what she was talking about. Unfortunately, these are only useful if the hair you are attempting to confine to the metal strip is small in diameter. I have solid beads and rings that can easily fit my hair and stay in place, yet I struggle with these.

I think the issue is that they are too flimsy to hold a large amount of hair in place if it is not already braided. I do not braid my hair, I just use beads to secure it in bunches. I use anything from a 5 mm inner diameter bead to a 17 mm inner diameter bead depending on how much hair I am attempting to secure. Unfortunately, these will not work for what I need them to do, but I believe if your hair is already braided that these can be worn like jewelry, just don't expect them to do any of the securing of your hair.

text:
I bought this as a blind buy, as there were no reviews on this fragrance.
I used to really like the original Candies, from back in the day. I was so upset when it was discontinued.
That said, my nostalgia got the best of me when I saw this on Amazon. I don’t recall what the original Candies smelled like, so its hard to say how close this is to the OG formulation, but this one smells really sweet. My initial response to it was “aqua net hairspray with citrus magic”. It is very ‘orangey’, and the citrus is huge here.
Once the hairspray smell goes away, it smells rather nice. It doesn’t smell expensive, you can tell it’s not a high end fragrance, but it’s nice. I wouldn’t buy another bottle, though. Same thing with the Candies Black (which I also bought- blindly). I wouldn’t renew either of these, but it was not a waste. They smell “OK” enough to wear out, and the projection is intense. These “new” Candies frags will make people around you get a headache, if you take a bath in them. A few sprays is literally all you need. Both are extremely strong, and the scent lasts a while on the skin, and a very long time on clothing.
It has a little bit of a gourmand thing going on after it dries...it smells like lemans and oranges. I suppose wanting to eat it is why it’s called “Candies”, so in that regard, kudos.
There’s something going on beneath all the citrus, but I cannot for the life of me put my finger on it.
Just know this is very sweet, like Invuctus, but “cheaper” smelling. It’s really not terrible, but they’re not for me.
A teenager may love these Candies fragrances, but if you are over 30, you may want to think again.

text:
This device is very similar to wax warming devices. It matches some higher end wax warmers, with the variable temperature control (some low cost wax warmers have an on/off switch only). The aluminum warming pot seems to have thinner wall thickness, though. The warming/melting pot is very light weight.
Wax warmers, with variable temperature controls, range in price $20 - $60 (with some on sale for under $20, and some 'professional' level units considerably more expensive). So, at $50, this is close to the upper end of the price range.
This warmer does heat up and maintains the chosen temperature. We tested the warming device with massage oil, and it does a credible job. A nice touch is a red light indicates the device is in the heating process to attain the selected temperature. It turns green, when it's at the selected temperature, giving a 'green light' to use the warmed material.
Overall, it's a competent warming device that seems a bit pricey. It does meet expectations, earning a 3.75 - 4 star rating.

text:
These are the first set of cryosticks I’ve ever used and, overall, I think the idea is as great one. The rejuvenating feeling of icy coldness can’t be beat, especially as a way to combat redness\swelling from allergies. These are very smooth and slide across the skin without any issues. I also appreciate that it’s a set of 2, since I keep them in the freezer when not in use and can easily switch between them.

Now for the reason I give these 3 stars: they are terribly balanced and have teeny, tiny handles. The globe part that contains the liquid is sort of heavy and very difficult to manage. After a few minutes of struggling, my fingers and thumb are sore from having to holding on so tightly to a stem that is less in circumference than a pen.

It’s a really silly and short-sighted weakness in an otherwise nice item.

text:
The scrubber tip on this tube of cleanser sounded like a great idea, so I ordered it. I’ve used charcoal face products on my very dry, sensitive skin in the past with no irritation or reaction & this one is fine, too. Cleans, rinses well, moisturizes a bit.

BUT — the angled tip has teeny nylon bristles that are actually too short. You squeeze the tube to push some product onto the surface & gently “scrub” your face with it. The problem is that the bristles are so short that it just feels like a plastic disc massage! No matter how gently or firmly I applied it, the result was the same - rubbing creamy gray cleanser across my face with a circle of hard plastic.

In theory, this idea is good but the actual design of the tip does not work for me.

**Thank you for reading my review & I hope that it’s Helpful!**

text:
I'll be the first to tell you: I'm not a huge fan of super "girly" scents. I don't like smelling like a baby prostitute or like an English garden of roses or gardenias or any of those other perfume-y flowers.

When I tell you that I am OBSESSED with this Cocoa Rose scented oil, I'm not lying. It's a perfect blend of rich notes and floral notes (but not too overwhelming). I think it's because it's an oil and not an actual perfume. I think if it were a scent, it would be too overpowering, but as a body oil, it's perfect. My boyfriend (who is NOT a fan of scents on me) asked me what I was wearing the first time I put this on and told me I smelled "delicious". WAT?

This stuff is so good, I bought the Malibu scent too. Do yourself a favor and BUY THEM BOTH AND THEN MIX THEM ONTO YOUR SKIN. YOU'LL THANK ME LATER.

In fact, I think I need a patent on that idea...

text:
I bought this for my daughter to use for her dance recital. Her hair is short and very thin, so it won’t be able to go around the mesh type donut without the mesh showing through.
I have two main complaints with this piece.
1) The blonde color looks too bleached out and consequently is very plastic looking... Think Barbie doll hair. (I was prepared for that at this price point though.)
2) The hole in the middle is HUGE! I can stick my whole thumb through it! You will have no choice but to put a ponytail through the middle for it to look like a somewhat normal bun. This is my biggest problem as I’m not sure my little one has enough hair to make it work.
This will serve its purpose, but I am glad it doesn’t need to be used very often and won’t be viewed too closely.
